Title: GWAS of grain color and tannin content in Chinese sorghum based on whole-genome sequencing

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed candidate transporter/trafficking gene associated with exocarp color, testa pigment, and tannin content
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Located within an important chromosome 4 QTL near the TAN1 region. 2. Encodes a GST ortholog of Arabidopsis AT5G41220. 3. Proposed as a possible candidate for the sorghum S gene involved in vacuolar/cellular deposition of tannins/pigments.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS colocalization across multiple environments/models; orthology annotation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang (2018, 2019, 2020) and Ledong (2020)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate gene only; not functionally validated in this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker candidate for breeding sorghum with desired pigment/tannin deposition traits for liquor brewing.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed candidate transporter for proanthocyanidin/tannin sequestration associated with grain color/tannin QTL
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Located near qtl8.60826776. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis TT12 MATE transporter. 3. Proposed to mediate transport of proanthocyanidins/tannins to the vacuole in sorghum.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang and Ledong
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate gene only; transporter role inferred from orthologous function.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for improving tannin storage/deposition patterns in brewing sorghum.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: B-ring hydroxylation branch of flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid substrates to provide 3'-hydroxylated flavonoid intermediates required for downstream pigment/tannin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate structural gene associated with exocarp color variation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Colocalized with qtl1.64009126 detected across all environments. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis TT7. 3. Proposed as a candidate structural gene affecting grain pigmentation/tannin-related flavonoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS colocalization across environments; orthology annotation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang (2018–2020) and Ledong (2020)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate only; no enzyme assay in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker candidate for modifying flavonoid branch flux and grain color in sorghum breeding.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: B-ring hydroxylation branch of flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid intermediates for downstream pigment/tannin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate structural gene associated with grain color QTL
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Colocalized with qtl8.54420851. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis TT7. 3. Proposed as a candidate structural gene contributing to grain pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang and Ledong
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for grain color/tannin-related breeding.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: B-ring hydroxylation branch of flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid intermediates for downstream pigment/tannin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate structural gene associated with exocarp color QTL
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Colocalized with qtl10.58403535. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis TT7. 3. Proposed as a candidate structural gene for grain color/tannin-related metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang and Ledong
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for modifying pigmentation-related flavonoid flux.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; TT4 ortholog
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes chalcone formation as a key entry reaction in flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate structural gene linked to tannin/pigment biosynthesis QTL
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Colocalized with qtl2.13453215. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis TT4. 3. Proposed as a key structural candidate in sorghum tannin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang and Ledong
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry step (inferred from CHS function)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate only; no direct biochemical validation in sorghum here.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target to alter overall flavonoid/tannin production.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Central oxidation steps of flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proposed to participate in flavanone hydroxylation or leucoanthocyanidin oxidation/reduction-linked steps associated with tannin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate structural gene associated with important QTL near TAN2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Corresponds to qtl2.9341745. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis TT6/TT18-related functions. 3. Proposed as a candidate structural gene in sorghum tannin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang and Ledong
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional assignment uncertain because orthology points to TT6 or TT18-related enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for fine-tuning downstream flavonoid composition.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls grain pericarp color and is associated with tannin content; Hap2 is linked to light-colored grains and lowest tannin content
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Y1 locus was repeatedly detected by GWAS for exocarp color, mesocarp color, and tannin content in all environments. 2. Haplotype analysis identified three major haplotypes defined by three nonsynonymous SNPs in exon 2. 3. Hap2 accessions had light-colored grains and the lowest tannin content, whereas Hap3 showed the highest tannin content.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS across multiple models/environments; haplotype analysis with phenotype comparison
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid/pigment biosynthesis regulating grain pericarp coloration and associated tannin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation (inferred from MYB TF identity)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major regulator of sorghum kernel pericarp color and associated tannin variation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang (2018–2020) and Ledong (2020)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A previously reported 3.2-kb deletion allele was discussed from prior studies but not resolved in this panel using current alignment strategy.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker/haplotype target for selecting grain color and tannin content in liquor-brewing sorghum.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower tannin may reduce suitability for some brewing uses but improve food palatability.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential regulator associated with pigmented testa and tannin accumulation; a new recessive allelic variant causing premature stop codon abolishes testa pigmentation and tannins
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TAN2 locus was detected by GWAS for exocarp color, mesocarp color, testa color, and tannin content across all environments/models. 2. Haplotype analysis identified seven major haplotypes with 17 mutation sites. 3. Hap3 carries a C/T mutation in exon 8 causing premature transcription termination, producing colorless testa, white pericarp, and no tannins. 4. The study discovered this new recessive TAN2 allelic variant in Chinese sorghum.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS across multiple models/environments; haplotype analysis; sequence variant-to-phenotype association
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Regulation of proanthocyanidin/condensed tannin biosynthesis and testa pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation (inferred from bHLH TF identity)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex component with TAN1/WD40 and a putative MYB factor
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator required for pigmented testa and condensed tannin synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang (2018–2020) and Ledong (2020)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes TAN2 as a core known regulator and reports a novel recessive allele in Chinese sorghum.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value diagnostic target for breeding sorghum with different tannin levels; key locus for marker-assisted selection in liquor-brewing programs.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss-of-function alleles eliminate tannins, which may reduce bird resistance, disease protection, and brewing quality traits.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed candidate MYB regulator for tannin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Colocalized with important qtl3.48904117. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis TT2 and rice MYB61. 3. Proposed as one of the likely missing MYB members of the sorghum MBW complex regulating tannin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Proposed MBW complex member with TAN2 and TAN1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate missing MYB regulator in sorghum proanthocyanidin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang and Ledong
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate regulator only; not functionally validated.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for discovering causal MYB alleles controlling tannin level.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Most likely candidate for the missing MYB regulator of sorghum tannin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Located in a significant chromosome 4 QTL detected in three environments using three models. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis TT2. 3. Highlighted by the authors as the most likely candidate MYB component missing from the sorghum MBW complex.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS colocalization in multi-environment/multi-model analysis; orthology annotation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Proposed MBW complex member with TAN2 and TAN1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong candidate MYB switch for tannin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang and Ledong
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate only; no expression or transactivation validation in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for marker-assisted selection or future functional validation to manipulate tannin content.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed candidate for the sorghum I (intensifier) gene affecting grain pigmentation intensity
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Located in qtl1.64009126 detected in all environments. 2. Encodes a putative bHLH protein. 3. Proposed as one of three likely candidate loci for the intensifier (I) gene influencing pericarp color intensity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS colocalization across environments; comparative functional inference
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Regulation of pigmentation intensity in flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Possibly MBW-related or competitive bHLH mechanism
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally important candidate controller of pigment intensity
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang and Ledong
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate inferred by homology to maize intensifier1 concept; not experimentally proven.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May help fine-tune grain color intensity independent of bulk tannin level.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed candidate regulator for grain pigmentation intensity/tannin-related traits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Colocalized with qtl3.2837597. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis EGL1 and rice OsbHLH025. 3. Proposed as one of the candidate loci for the sorghum I gene.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang and Ledong

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate regulator associated with testa color and proposed I-gene function
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Colocalized with qtl7.61701838 linked to testa color. 2. Orthologous to Arabidopsis TT8. 3. Proposed as a candidate regulator for grain color intensity and tannin-related pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments in Guiyang and Ledong
